title: chenopodium nutans subsp. oxycarpum
intro: Chenopodium nutans subsp. oxycarpum, also known as Klamath weed or Stinking goosefoot, is a plant species in the Chenopodiaceae family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids, specifically oxycardine. The health benefits of Chenopodium nutans subsp. oxycarpum include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis. Chenopodium nutans subsp. oxycarpum can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use.
